10. Direct the Municipal Accountant to identify the causes and differences between these reciprocal accounts and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books.  

11. Henceforth, conduct regular review of reciprocal accounts and adjust accordingly.

12. Direct management to exert more effort to enforce massive and strategic collections of delinquent real property taxes by frequently conducting tax campaigns and offering incentives to full payment of tax dues; or impose the available remedies provided for under the Local Government Code.

13. Require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to resolve the land issues and other issues affecting the above development projects for its immediate completion.  

14. Henceforth, ensure permits and clearances are obtained during the project preparation phase and adhere to the provisions of Annex A of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184  to avoid delays and achieve the timely completion of the projects ready for use by its constituents to positively promote its good governance.

21. Direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize sale based on Official Receipt and record them as income in the books. Also, require the General Services Officer/ Municipal Treasurer to forward to the Municipal Accountant the necessary documents constituting the sale and for the latter to make the necessary adjustments in the books.

22. Require management to furnish us with a written explanation to the deficiencies noted above and submit the necessary lacking documents. The improper and incomplete documentation of the foregoing transactions rendered the transactions doubtful as to its validity and existence, thus, if the lacking documents will not be submitted and could not be explained, the transactions will be suspended in audit and eventually be disallowed if remained undocumented and unjustified.

23. Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and other implementing offices to review the composition of the 20% DF continuing appropriation, identify the savings of completed projects and those which were no longer prioritized and, subject to the provisions of the Local Government Code and the DILG-DBM JMC, re-appropriate to current/existing priority development projects of the 20% DF. We also recommend that the MPDC and other implementing offices coordinate and strategize to optimally utilize the 20% DF and regularly monitor the status of utilization and implementation of PPAs.

24. Request for Authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off those accounts determined uncollectible and doubtful, pursuant to Sec. 36 of PD 1445. The request shall be supported by documents enumerated in Section 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 following the procedures provided in Section 8 (Procedures on the write-off of dormant accounts) of the same COA Circular.

25. Require management to strictly adhere with the provision of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. Furthermore, management should require the concerned officers and employees to settle immediately their outstanding cash advances by demanding immediate submission of duly accomplished liquidation reports with complete supporting documents.

26. Require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books of the municipality dropping the cost of the structures and transfer the same to the books of the recipient barangays. Turn-over documents of newly completed structure/s have to be attached to the JEV transferring the cost of the structure/s.

27. Direct management to adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2007-001 to ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of fund transfers to NGOs/POs. 

28. Management has to institute action against NGOs/POs and their officers/ organizers that failed to implement the programs and projects funded by the agency pursuant to the provisions of the Memorandum of Agreement covering these transactions.

30 Direct the Municipal Accountant to ascertain whether or not these payable accounts are still due for remittance to concerned officers. If these are still due for remittance, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the amount involved in order to protect the interest of the employee-members concerned and clear the books of these unremitted payable accounts.

31. Require the inventory committee to prioritize the conduct of a complete inventory count of all properties and submit the corresponding reports to the auditor in accordance with Section 124 of Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS), Volume I.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer give priority to settling the above reconciling items in order to present an accurate and reliable Cash In Bank Account balance in the financial statements;
The Municipal Treasurer settle the service charge amounting ₱1,000.00 as it is his personal liability.
The Municipal Treasurer send a written notice to the payee of the check at least one month before it becomes stale, and cancel and restore back to the cash account when the checks become stale in observance with Sections 61 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs on the proper treatment and accounting procedures for stale checks.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to trace and analyze the factors contributing to the occurrence of the noted unreconciled difference in the Cash-Local Treasury and Cash in Bank-LCCA accounts and effect proper adjustments in both records. Further, we recommended that both Department Heads reconcile their cash records at least quarterly to ensure reliability and accuracy of the balances recorded.

We recommended and management agreed that the Inventory Committee to prioritize and ensure full implementation of the “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Agriculturist prepare and maintain the ledger cards and stock/property cards respectively, for the Breeding Stocks and reconcile their balances regularly,  and the Municipal Agriculturist facilitate the proper valuation of these assets to reflect the account's accurate value.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant re-evaluate each payable account to ascertain the existence of actual claims and send confirmations to the claimants so that the required reversion of items, which had been in the books for two years or more, would be effected.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant record the sale of bidding documents under Trust Liability account which shall be used for honorarium and overtime pay of the BAC.  The necessary correcting journal entry/ies shall also be effected to transfer the any unutilized balance from the Trust Liabilities (Trust Fund) to the General Fund.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the suppliers to issue Official Receipts or Sales Invoices in the procurement of goods and services and adhere to the provision of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended and Management agreed that the BAC, in coordination with the BAC Secretariat, develop additional measures in advertising bid invitations, such as posting on the Municipality’s website and its own social media accounts to attract more prospective bidders.

We recommended that management instruct the BAC Secretariat to prepare and submit the APP that incorporates all PPMPs for the year. Also, we recommended that management ensure that any changes made are incorporated into the APP and shall be done every six months or as often as necessary to reflect the actual procurement plan for the year.

We recommended and Management agreed that the BAC prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) in the prescribed format and submit the same to the GPPB within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of the semester as required under Section 12.2 of RA 9184 and its IRR to enable the GPPB to perform its duty, among others, of evaluating the effectiveness of the government procurement system and recommending improvements in its system and procedures.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer immediately process the application of fidelity bond of the job order employee who is acting as a collecting officer in compliance with the bonding requirement stated in Treasury Circular No. 02-009 dated August 6, 2009.

We recommended that and Management agreed that they shall:
Designate another qualified Special Disbursing Officer if additional cash advances are necessary and justified under the circumstances; or
Increase the amount of fidelity bond of the Disbursing Officer to sufficiently cover his cash accountabilities.
We recommended that management re-evaluate the controls in the procurement of gasoline and effect necessary procedures to ensure that possible errors and irregularities are eliminated.

1. Require the Municipal Accountant to established RPT and SET Receivables for the current year based on the report of the Municipal Treasurer showing the list of the taxpayers and the amount due to accurately present the RPT/SET Receivable accounts in the financial statements as of December 31, 2022.

2. Require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments reverting the set-up of receivable amounts pertaining to uncollected rentals on block tienda to present fairly the financial statements of the LGU as of December 31, 2022.  Henceforth, adhere to the afore-cited provisions Section 19 (c), Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs in the accounting for income.

3. Direct the Sangguniang Bayan to stop hiring job order and contract of service personnel without proper authorization from the Local Chief Executive pursuant to Sections 77, 444(b)(1)(vi) and 22(c) of RA 7160. Moreover, ensure that contracts entered into by the LCE are duly authorized by the local sanggunian.

4. Direct management to review other agency’s functions, systems and procedures, organizational structure, and staffing pattern to be able to address any needs and gaps in the human resource complement of the agency. 

5. Comply with all the provisions of the JC and consider long existing qualified COS/JO workers when filling all its vacant plantilla positions.

6. Submit copy of the APMV for the purchase of multicab as required in Section 7 of DBM BC NO. 2022-1. We also recommend management to seek consideration from the DBM on the purchase of surplus motor vehicle to justify the procurement in violation of Section 9 of the same DBM BC No. 2022-1 prohibiting the purchase of reconditioned motor vehicles.

7. Direct the Supply Officer to maintain a detailed inventory records (Supplies Ledger Card) for each inventory item.  
8. Adhere strictly to the provisions of Sec. 121, Vol. I of the NGAS Manual in the preparation of reports on the requisitions and issuances of inventories.  Report of all issuances shall then be furnished to the Municipal Accountant to recognize expenses on all issued inventories.

9. Require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the cost of inventory actually remained in stock.

15.Finalize the revision/update of the revenue code pursuant to Section 191 of RA 7160 through revisiting articles and provisions of the Municipal’s existing Revenue Code series of 2011 considering present business environment towards the best interest of the public and the government.  

16. We also recommend that enactment of the revised revenue code be made immediately so the potential increase in collections will be achieve to provide additional funds which can be used to finance programs and projects of LGU.

17. Municipal officials have to see to it that all lessees of the public market stalls and other LGU owned properties for rent are covered with a contract of lease embodying therein the terms and conditions in accordance with COA Circular No. 88-282A to ensure that interests of both parties are protected in case ambiguities and/or misunderstandings occurred.

18. Direct management to execute a Memorandum of Agreement for all transfers of funds to other LGUs pursuant to Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 stipulating therein all the conditions agreed by the parties to the contract to facilitate the implementation of the intended purpose of the fund.

19. Require the Market Administrator/Municipal Treasurer to enforce the collection of overdue accounts of market stall occupants and impose sanctions provided in the Municipality’s Local Revenue Code.

20. Require the Municipal Accountant to set up the depreciation and Accumulated depreciation of these assets and to make the necessary adjusting journal entries recognizing the unprovided depreciation and amortization expenses for CY2021 to present fairly the financial statements of the municipality.
32. We recommend that the amount granted as PEI in CY 2014 be refunded by the officials and employees.

33. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to require or demand for the issuance of an official receipt of the government by the recipient League concerned in order to support the release of financial aid to the latter, pursuant to Section 69 of the GAAM, Volume I.

34. Require the League concerned to submit a copy of their Constitution and By-laws as basis of reviewing bodies in determining the propriety of the fund release.

35. Further advice all Leagues through its officers to purchase and use the Official Receipt of Republic of the Philippines.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management strictly adhere with the provision of COA
Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the granting, utilization and
liquidation of cash advances. Furthermore, management should require the concerned
officers and employees to settle immediately their outstanding cash advances by
demanding immediate submission of duly accomplished liquidation reports with
complete supporting documents.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the accounting guidelines
set forth in Section 104 Vol. I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs to ensure fair
presentation of the financial statements.

We require the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the proper recording of
transactions in accordance with the Chart of Accounts as prescribed in COA Circular
No. 2015-009, to be able to maintain accurate and reliable accounting records, and to
ensure that the financial statements of the municipality are presented fairly.

We require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the
books of the municipality dropping the cost of the structures and transfer the same to
the books of the recipient barangays. Turn-over documents of newly completed
structure/s have to be attached to the JEV transferring the cost of the structure/s.

Require the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the proper and correct
classification of accounts. Use of journal entries as well as preparation of the financial
statements shall always be in accordance with the Chart of Accounts as prescribed in
COA Circular No. 2015-009, to be able to maintain accurate and reliable accounting
records, and to ensure that the financial statements of the municipality are presented
fairly. Consequently, require the adjustment of the above enumerated accounts to its
appropriate account titles.

We recommend that management adhere to the provisions of COA Circular
2007-001 to ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of fund transfers to
NGOs/POs.
Management has to institute action against NGOs/POs and their officers/
organizers that failed to implement the programs and projects funded by the agency
pursuant to the provisions of the Memorandum of Agreement covering these
transactions.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to use the appropriate accounts in
recording the transfer of unexpended LDRRMF pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-
009.

In view of the Supreme Court’s decision pronouncing the unconstitutionality of
the PDAF, we recommend that the remaining funds shall no longer be released/ used
but shall be returned to the Bureau of Treasury so that it can be used for other priority
programs of the government.

We recommend that petty cash custodians replenish their petty cash fund when
75% of the fund has already been used up or as needed.

We recommend management to accomplish the prescribed Driver’s Trip Ticket
and submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Official Travels as required
in COA Circular No. 77-61.

We recommend that management adhere to the guidelines set forth by DILG
and DBM in the utilization of its 20% Development Fund in order to ensure that
government funds are properly utilized.

We require the municipal officials concerned to adhere to the requirements of
COA Circular 98-002 in the hiring of private lawyer as their legal counsel. They have to
exhaust all means to seek assistance from the Provincial Legal Office to handle
their cases. In the event that a government lawyer is not available, a written
conformity from the Solicitor General with concurrence from the Commission on
Audit has to be first secured before hiring the services of a private lawyer.

We recommend that management optimally use the 70% allocation of the
LDRRMF and fully implement projects and activities that are under the four (4)
thematic areas, namely, disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness,
disaster response and disaster rehabilitation and recovery as provided in Section 5.0 of
NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommend that management follow the guidelines for the grant of CNA to
avoid suspension or disallowance of transaction.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee strictly adhere to the
provisions in the RIRR of RA 9184 to prevent the transaction from being suspended/
disallowed in audit.

Explanation is requested for the above noted violations.

We require management to submit the lacking documents in order to avoid suspension of this transaction and ensure that future transactions are complete with necessary documents before effecting payment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend  that  management  require  the  inventory  committee  to
prioritize the conduct of a complete inventory count of all  properties  and
submit the corresponding reports to the auditor in accordance  with  Section
124 of Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS), Volume I.

It is recommended that management release  financial  assistance  only
when the documentary requirements are complete  in  order  to  avoid  future
disallowances and  comply  with  established  laws  and  regulations  before
effecting payment pursuant to Section  4  of  DSWD  Memorandum  Circular  01
dated February 17, 2014.

We recommend management to stop the practice of specifying  an  amount
to be released to the indigents seeking for financial assistance and  adhere
to DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 09 series of 2016 in the grant of  financial
assistance.

We recommend that management ensure  that  disbursement  vouchers  are
properly verified and signed pursuant to Section 4(5)  and  (6)  of  PD  No.
1445. Management should also provide  an  explanation  of  the  above  noted
deficiency and certify that the DVs listed in Annex A are valid,  legal  and
proper  transactions,  whenever  applicable.  Otherwise,  the  disbursements
could be considered not compliant with the fundamental principle  and  could
be suspended in audit.

We recommend that management require the Local School Board to  adhere
to the provisions of Sec. 272 of RA 7160 in identifying the transactions  or
projects to be funded by the Special Education Fund.  We  further  recommend
that management stop the practice of providing  load  allowances  and  other
expenses which are not among those identified to be funded from the  SEF  to
avoid disallowance is audit.

We recommend that management evaluate the status of  their  job  order
employees  and  recommend  for  creation   of   permanent   positions   when
applicable. Furthermore,  management  needs  to  immediately  revisit  their
vacant plantilla positions and consider the employees with job order  status
with long years of service for such vacant positions.

We  recommend  the  Bids  and  Awards  Committee  to  adhere  to  the
procurement process as outlined in RIRR RA  9184  to  avoid  suspension  and
disallowance of transactions.

It is recommended that management review every contract  entered  into
with the supplier and ensure that the conditions  and  terms  stipulated  in
the contract are met.  Further,  require  the  supplier  to  pay  liquidated
damages pursuant to the provisions in  the  R-IRR  of  RA  9184  to  prevent
disallowance in audit.

It is recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) provide  an
explanation to the above noted deficiencies. The BAC  is  also  reminded  to
adhere to the provisions of the procurement  law  embodied  in  RA  9184  to
avoid disallowance of transactions.

It is recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) provide  an
explanation in their failure to require  party  of  a  Performance  Security
before contract  signing.  The  BAC  is  also  reminded  to  adhere  to  the
provisions of the procurement law embodied in RA 9184 to avoid  disallowance
of transactions.

It is recommended that  management  look  into  this  irregularity  of
project construction and submit an explanation as to the  blatant  delay  of
these BEA projects’ completion. Management should also get  a  copy  of  the
Statement of Work Accomplished as of date  of  completion  and  compute  for
liquidated damages.

It is  recommended  that  management  submit  the  lacking/unsubmitted
documents and provide explanation for the inconsistencies noted in order  to
prevent the above transactions from being suspended/  disallowed  in  audit.
The concerned officials must adhere to the provisions of Section 4(6) of  PD
No. 1445 that all claims/ payments against the  government  fund  should  be
completely supported with the necessary documents for the  validity  of  the
transactions paid.

Stop the practice of indicating brand names and  specific  description
of the items in the Purchase Request and Request  for  Quotations.  Pursuant
to Section 18 of RA 9184, specifications for the procurement of goods should
be  based  on  the  relevant  characteristics   and/or   performance
requirements of the item/s.

      Adhere to the afore-cited provision of RA 9184 to avoid disallowance
in audit in the future transactions of the LGU.

We recommend that the Local Disaster  Risk  Reduction  and  Management
Officer submit  a  Report  on  Sources  and  Utilization  of  Disaster  Risk
Reduction Management Fund (DRRMF) on or before the 15th day  after  the  end
of each month.  We  also  would  like  to  highlight  to  management  to  be
continually guided by the provisions and laws regarding the  allocation  and
utilization of LDRRMF.

We recommend that Management maintains a detailed inventory records (Supplies Ledger Card) for each inventory item.  Adhere strictly to the provisions of Sec. 04(j), Vol. I of NGAS Manual.
We further recommend the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the cost of inventory actually remained in stock.
